notes: Spain dominated with 27 shots but Vozinha (40yo GK) made 7 saves. Cape Verde pure deep block delivered exactly as modelled.
match_notes: Spain had 27 shots, 70% possession, xG 2.29 vs 0.3. Cape Verde completed only 14 passes in opposition half. Pure deep block confirmed — never threatened on counter. 10 Spain corners came from sustained wide probing across 90 mins. Tier 1 setup delivered perfectly.

notes: Egypt scored in 19th min via Ashour. Belgium chased but Egypt dominated corners on the counter. AH corners destroyed.
match_notes: Egypt goal at 19 mins completely flipped corners battle. Belgium pushed forward, Egypt sat deep and launched counters winning corners at the other end. Final corners: Belgium 2 Egypt 7. Lukaku OG equalised 66 mins but damage done. AH corners Belgium -3 was never going to land once Egypt scored early. Counter-threat via Salah and Marmoush confirmed deadly.

notes: Saudi scored first, Uruguay chased desperately. 18 corners — only 1 in first 36 mins then 17 in remainder. Game state explosion.
match_notes: Only 1 corner in first 36 minutes then 17 in the remainder after Saudi scored. Uruguay attempted 22 shots in second period — most in one half at WC since 1974. Goalkeeper Alowais was outstanding. This is the definitive proof that underdog scores early = favourite chases wide = corners explode. Direct team assumption completely wrong when game state flips.

notes: Two direct physical teams. Only 5 corners despite both needing a win. Style beat urgency completely.
match_notes: Iran and New Zealand are both direct physical sides with no wide possession pattern. Despite both needing a result only 5 corners produced. NZ have now drawn 4 successive WC matches as underdogs — resilient defensive shape. Must-win urgency had zero effect on corner volume. Style is the only thing that matters.

notes: France wide possession vs Senegal compact mid-block. Tight first half then opened up. 10 corners matched baseline of 10.6.
match_notes: France dominated territory through wide threats Dembele, Olise, Barcola. Senegal sat in compact mid-block with counter-threat but France's width was too sustained. Tight first half as expected then corners built across 90 mins. Senegal got 4 corners themselves showing they weren't purely passive. Baseline 10.6 was accurate. Counter-threat present but edge 1.1 above line was enough — v4.1 rule validated.

notes: Haaland scored twice through central route. Norway dominant but low corners because they score through middle not wide.
match_notes: Haaland scored twice and Norway dominated but generated only 4 corners. Norway's attacking pattern is central — Haaland holds up play, Odegaard plays through the middle. Iraq set-piece specialist style added minor corners. This confirmed the central possession rule: scoring through the middle = low corners even in dominant wins. Blowout via central scorer pattern.

notes: Messi hat trick. Argentina won through individual brilliance centrally. Algeria offered nothing. Only 4 corners in a 3-0 blowout.
match_notes: Argentina 3-0 via Messi hat trick — pure individual central brilliance. Once Argentina went 2-0 up early they stopped probing wide and managed the game. Algeria counter_attacking style had no outlet to generate corners themselves. Blowout via central scorer = corners collapse confirmed again. 4 corners in 90 mins is the floor for this match type.

notes: Austria won comfortably. Jordan debut side but managed 3 corners themselves. Blowout pattern kept corners moderate at 7.
match_notes: Austria 3-1 via direct physical approach. Jordan debut side performed better than expected winning 3 corners themselves. Austria scored through direct route not sustained wide crossing. Blowout pattern meant Austria eased off in second half reducing corner volume. 7 corners consistent with central/direct dominant win pattern.

notes: Joao Neves 6th min, Wissa equalised 45+5. Congo scored in first half stoppage time from cross. Portugal never fully dominated.
match_notes: Portugal scored at 6 mins but Wissa equalised at 45+5 from Arthur Masuaku cross — headed home. Congo scored right before half time so game state was level from the start of second half. Portugal had only 5 corners in 90 mins despite wide possession attack style. Congo's counter-threat via Wissa was real and decisive — he had a chance in 10th min too. LOW data confidence + counter-threat = confirmed skip signal. 9 corners right at line.

notes: Colombia won 3-1 via Luis Diaz and Campaz second half goals. Central possession style confirmed low corners at altitude.
match_notes: Colombia scored through Luis Diaz and Jaminton Campaz in second half — both central attacking moves not wide crosses. Uzbekistan debut side performed defensively as expected. Altitude at Azteca reduced tempo significantly. 7 corners total confirms altitude -1.0 modifier and central possession low corner profile. Under lean was correct — line was 9.0, actual was 7.

notes: Ghana won 1-0 via late Yirenkyi goal. Only 4 corners total. Baseline at line was correct signal to skip.
match_notes: Ghana won via late goal but generated only 2 corners despite needing to break down Panama. Panama sat deep and compact — but Ghana are counter_attacking/direct not wide possession. Both teams physical and direct = low corners regardless of must-win urgency. 4 corners is the floor. Baseline at line was perfectly priced by bookmaker.

notes: England 4-2 dominant win. 8 England corners from sustained wide pressure. Saka, Gordon, Madueke all wide threat confirmed.
match_notes: England generated 8 corners through Saka, Gordon, Madueke wide delivery. Croatia sat deep as expected with Modric at 40 in deeper role. No counter-threat from Croatia — zero pace in behind. HIGH data confidence + no counter-threat + wide possession = clean Tier 2 delivered. 10 corners matched baseline of 10.0 exactly. England dynamic and exciting — multiple wide outlets all active.

notes: Sadilek scored 6th min, Czechia then created nothing for 80 minutes (xG 1.02 vs SA 1.37). Mokoena penalty equalised 83rd min after deflected long shot off Sulc arm. Even 5-5 corners reflect a back-and-forth tactical match.
match_notes: Czechia went ahead through Sadilek in 6th minute but were toothless after — Schick missed two free headers, Darida fluffed a clear chance. Their early-goal management style means they stop generating going forward but their throw-in/set-piece game (Coufal, Sadilek corners early) keeps corner count ticking even in a passive second half. South Africa improved after suspension overhaul, pushed numbers forward chasing the game from ~60min onward, matching corners 5-5 despite missing 2 starters. Set piece specialist style underperformed expected dominance; baseline of 8.0 was too low, actual 10 corners beat it.

notes: Goalless and tight for 70 minutes despite Swiss dominance. Match exploded in final 20 mins: Manzambi brace off the bench (71,90), Vargas (84), Mahmic for Bosnia (90+3), Xhaka pen (90+7). Muharemovic red card for Bosnia at 84th min.
match_notes: Switzerland generated corners steadily throughout via Rieder/Aebischer/Alajbegovic-conceded set pieces even while goalless - wide_possession identity held even against a stubborn Bosnia low block. Bosnia's resistance (35 clearances in 1st half alone) capped their own corner output at 3, consistent with compact_mid_block/deep_block profile. Game state flip (red card + late goals) came too late to meaningfully inflate corner count, since corners are mostly a buildup-phase stat, not a chaos-phase stat.

notes: Bet placed: Total Over 9.5 @ 1.800, stake 1.00u - WON (+0.80u). Qatar red card #1 at 34' (Homam Ahmed, pulled down Buchanan, Canada already 2-0 up). Red card #2 at 54' (Madibo, dangerous tackle that also stretchered off Kone with a serious leg injury) reduced Qatar to 9 men for the final 36+ minutes. Canada cruised to 6-0.
match_notes: Two separate red cards 20 minutes apart, both directly enabling sustained Canadian width and territorial domination - this wasn't one chaotic moment but a full second half of 9-vs-11 played out as a training-ground rondo. Qatar's solitary corner came in the first half before going down to 10 men. Canada's 19 corners is an extreme outlier even for a strong wide_possession team (their match 1 baseline was ~9 corners vs a full-strength Bosnia).

notes: Quinones and Jimenez scored. 3 red cards (2 SA 1 Mexico late). Played at Estadio Azteca altitude. Low corners despite dominance.
match_notes: Mexico dominant 1.41xG vs 0.07. SA went to 9 men (Sithole 50th). Mexico lost Montes 90+2. Low corner output at altitude with stretched defensive match after red cards.

notes: Czechia scored first via Krejci header from Hlozek long throw (59th min). Korea came back via Hwang In-beom and Oh Hyeon-gyu (80th sub for Son).
match_notes: Czechia long throw-in scoring identity confirmed independently — second data point after SA match. Korea comeback driven by subs and central combination play.

notes: OG (Bobadilla 7th) + Balogun brace (31st 45+5) + Reyna late. USA 602 vs 316 passes 23 vs 5 crosses but only 3 corners from total control.
match_notes: USA scored through individual quality and turnovers not sustained wide crossing despite dominant box score. Central_possession identity confirmed.

notes: Bosnia led via Lukic header from Basic corner (21st). Larin equalized (78th sub). Canada 9 corners via sustained wide pressure Eustaquio taking corners.
match_notes: Representative Canada form — 9 corners through sustained width without Davies. Bosnia set-piece goal from own corner confirms their compact_mid_block identity.

notes: Embolo penalty 17th for Switzerland. Khoukhi headed equalizer 90+4 from Homam Al Amin deep cross — Qatar first-ever WC point. Swiss 26 shots 3.24xG.
match_notes: Qatar TRUE representative baseline — held compact shape despite Swiss dominance. Switzerland 10 corners confirms wide_possession identity independently of Bosnia match.

notes: Saibari lobbed Alisson for Morocco lead. Vinicius Jr solo equalizer 32nd — cut inside curled top corner individual brilliance not wide buildup.
match_notes: Brazil leans central_possession — equalizer was pure individual quality. Morocco compact defending Bounou key saves. 6 Brazil corners from territory but goals from central quality.

notes: McGinn 28th min deflected shot from Gannon-Doak cutback. Haiti debutants competitive — Pierrot header just wide 84th. Scotland first WC win since 1990.
match_notes: Haiti 4 corners vs Scotland 3 shows genuine competitiveness from debutants. Not a passive minnow performance. Both teams generated corners through direct/central play.

notes: Turkiye dominated possession 8 corners but lost to two counter-attacking breaks — Irankunda pace solo run (27th) Metcalfe long-range turnover (75th).
match_notes: Classic counter-threat case: dominant possession team (Turkiye 8 corners) loses to clinical counter_attacking side. Australia low own corners but won the match. Separate corner output from match result.

notes: Comenencia scored 21st for Curacao before rout. Musiala/Havertz/Wirtz goals via central combination play. Schlotterbeck header from corner (38th) only wide-driven goal.
match_notes: Germany central_possession with set-piece contribution — 7 goals mostly from individual/combination quality not wide crossing. Curacao debut smallest nation ever in WC.

notes: Van Dijk header from Gravenberch cross (51st). Summerville solo curl (64th). Japan 88th-89th min equalizer DIRECTLY FROM CORNER — Ogawa header off Ito corner via Kamada.
match_notes: Japan late equalizer directly from corner confirms set_piece_specialist aerial profile. Netherlands wide play identity via van Dijk cross-header. Entertaining back-and-forth.

notes: Ecuador hit woodwork 4 times dominated first half but could not score. Amad Diallo 90th-min winner via Singo cutback — counter/individual finish not buildup.
match_notes: Ecuador 5 corners vs Ivory Coast 3 despite losing — same pattern as Turkiye/Australia. Dominant but inefficient possession team beaten by clinical counter. Ivory Coast counter_attacking confirmed.

notes: Ayari brace from distance Isak-Gyokeres combination sub scored 12 sec after on. All 5 goals via individual quality/long-range — zero wide-crossing goals.
match_notes: Classic central scorer blowout = low corners. 5-1 scoreline but only 6 total corners. Second independent confirmation of Argentina-Algeria lesson.

notes: Skip-lean under was wrong - baseline 8.0 vs line 9.0, actual came in at 11. USA produced 7 corners (vs MD1 rate of 3.0) while winning 2-0; Australia 4 (vs MD1 rate of 5.0) despite losing on the counter again.
Bet placed: Total Under 8.5 @ 1.900, stake 1.00u - WON (+0.90u). Baseline 5.0 vs line 8.5 (edge -3.5) held despite n=1 for both teams - the biggest gap of the day, and it delivered.

Skip correct in spirit - baseline 10.0 matched line 10.0 exactly (no lean given), actual came in at 8. Even 4-4 split despite the 3-0 scoreline - Brazil didn't dominate corners proportionally to the result.

Skip correct (edge was only +0.5, no real lean). Actual 12 corners beat the 8.5 line easily, but the split is the headline: Paraguay won 1-0 without a single corner - 0-for-2 in Group D, second straight match with near-zero attacking territory.

Skip correct (edge -0.5, no real lean) - actual 7 well under the 9.5 line. Netherlands took just 2 corners despite scoring 5; Sweden took 5 despite losing big - same low-output-favourite / high-output-chaser pattern as Australia, Türkiye, and now a fourth confirmation.

Bet placed: Total Under 9.0 @ 1.800, stake 1.00u - WON (+0.80u), taken below the 1.850 tier_2 floor as an explicit override given the edge size and multi-match research backing both teams. Tunisia took 3 corners even in a 4-goal loss, Japan capped at 5 despite total dominance (57% poss, 10 shots) - both consistent with the 6-match non-WC research from yesterday, not outlier blowout noise.

Bet placed: Total Under 9.0 @ 1.900, stake 1.00u - PUSH/VOID, total landed exactly on the whole-number line. Curacao took 0 corners despite conceding 26 shots and 75% possession to Ecuador - second straight match at near-zero output, fully confirms yesterday's research and the planned reliability upgrade.

Skip correct re: AH (CT forbade it, and Ivory Coast's 30th-minute Kessie goal would have destroyed an AH bet exactly per the model's own warning). But the Over lean would have WON - 11 well clear of the 9.5 line. CT killed the AH read appropriately but may have been overly conservative on the Over/Under total market specifically.

Bet placed: Total Over 10.0 @ 2.000, stake 1.00u - LOST (-1.00u). Spain dominated (21 shots, 65% poss) but scored via individual quality (Yamal solo run, Oyarzabal brace, OG) rather than sustained wide crossing - only 6 corners. Group-context check found no dead-rubber reason to expect underperformance, which is itself informative: the scoring mechanism mattered more than the stakes context.

Skip correct (no lean given) - actual 7 landed under the 9.0 line. Egypt's counter_attacking profile (CT=yes) held despite 56% possession - Salah (67') and Trezeguet (82') scored on the break, exactly the pattern that suppresses corners for both sides regardless of who's dominating the ball.

Skip-lean over was directionally correct but badly undersold the magnitude - actual 15 corners blew past both the 9.5 line and our own edge-adjusted estimate of 10.35. Uruguay alone took 11 corners against Cape Verde's pure_deep_block, well above their 7.6 prior average.

Skip-lean under confirmed - actual 6 well below the 9.5 line, despite real hesitation pre-match about Belgium's wide threats potentially getting inflated against a sitting Iran. Belgium went down to 10 men at 67' (Nathan Ngoy red card) and still couldn't break through - total stalemate.

Jordan led 1-0 at 36th min (underdog scored early), Algeria equalised 69th then won 82nd - favourite chased wide hard, racking up 10 corners to Jordan's 1. Classic underdog-scores-early corner explosion, just at a lower absolute volume than Saudi-Uruguay.

Messi scored 38th and 90+5th. Argentina central_possession identity held again - just 1 corner from the home side despite controlling the game (54% poss, 12 shots). Austria managed 3 corners themselves.

Mbappe 14th and 54th, Dembele 66th. France dominated (19 shots, 56% poss) but the blowout set in early and corners stayed low (6 total) despite France's wide_possession tag - thin edge (+0.65) on Over was taken anyway and lost.
